data.body:
PASS 1 review (T10 — Infrastructure / IaC overlay) for Cluster 2 (unify the two `_advisory_lock` backoff functions into one parameterized helper): NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS, no findings. (Recorded on the epic; prior Cluster 2 pass-1 reviews — T1, A1, E4, G6, G1G2, T5c — were likewise logged here.)

Scope check: T10 is an overlay that applies ONLY when a plan provisions or configures infrastructure (cloud resources; IaC such as Terraform/CloudFormation/CDK/Pulumi/Ansible; Kubernetes/Helm). This cluster is a pure in-file Python refactor of `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_advisory_lock.py` — collapsing two structurally-identical jittered-exponential-backoff functions (`_compute_backoff_seconds` at :126-137, `_cas_backoff_seconds` at :417-429) into one parameterized pure helper, preserving the formula, the RNG draw order, and both retry budgets. No infrastructure is provisioned or configured.

Per the overlay's own applicability gate ("apply only when the plan provisions or configures infrastructure; else PASS not-applicable") and its ANTI-FP guidance ("not-applicable for non-infra tickets"), none of the T10 sub-checks engage:
- STATE / remote-state + locking / plan-before-apply — N/A (no IaC state; the module's `.reconciler-pass-lock` is an in-repo advisory file, not Terraform/cloud state).
- LEAST-PRIVILEGE IAM / no wildcard grants / no long-lived creds — N/A (no roles/policies/credentials introduced).
- IDEMPOTENCY & DRIFT — N/A (no provisioned resources that could drift; behavior-preserving code change).
- BLAST RADIUS & ENV ISOLATION / destroy-replace safety / prevent_destroy — N/A (no stateful infra; nothing can be destroyed/replaced by an apply).
- SECRETS in IaC/vars — N/A (no IaC vars; stdlib-only, no secrets).
- COST & SIZING / autoscaling/quotas — N/A (no provisioned resources).
- OBSERVABILITY & OWNERSHIP / reproducible-as-code teardown — N/A (no new infra to monitor or tear down).

No T10 findings.

NOTE (NOT a T10 finding): prior passes (E4/A1/G6) already flagged that the plan's "What" section misstates the backoff constants relative to the live code. That is a behavior-preservation / premise-accuracy concern, not an infrastructure one, so it is not re-raised under T10.
